name: The Sovereign of the Void
tagline: Obsidian in a glass, ember in the finish.
description: A pitch-black obsidian pour built on activated charcoal and rye, cut with smoked rosemary and a bitter velvet amaro float. The rim is painted in crimson bitters reduction — the only color permitted to break the black. This is not a drink that asks to be liked. It asks to be understood.
method: Torch a rosemary sprig under a smoking cloche until the glass fills with ember-grey smoke.
In a mixing glass over a single obsidian-black sphere, combine rye, amaro, and smoked rosemary syrup.
Stir 40 rotations — slow, deliberate, no splash.
Paint the rim of a frozen Nick & Nora with crimson bitters reduction using a fine brush.
Strain into the glass beneath the still-lingering smoke, releasing the cloche tableside.
story: Conceived as the closing pour of a tasting menu — the drink that ends the night rather than continues it.

name: Black Widow
description: Wild blackberry muddled into dark rum and activated charcoal, finished with a single suspended flake of 24k gold leaf — beauty and danger sharing the same glass. Deep, fruit-forward, and unapologetically theatrical.
method: Muddle fresh blackberries at the bottom of a shaker tin.
Add rum, lime, demerara syrup, and activated charcoal over cubed ice.
Shake hard for 12 seconds — you want the charcoal fully suspended in the blackberry black.
Double strain into a coupe rimmed with gold leaf dust.
Float a single flake of gold leaf on the surface just before serving.
story: Named for its deceptive elegance — soft on the nose, sharp in the tail.
